expectations.text: Case focus is explicitly covered: modern grid hyperlink-style coverage separates contextual-link styling from ordinary element rendering
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly covers the separation: it states the focus requires separating “contextual-link-styled cells vs ordinary cells,” and proposes scenarios that contrast “Cells/values with contextual links (must render with hyperlink styling + indicator icon)” vs “Ordinary cells/values without contextual links (must not inherit hyperlink styling; render as standard text).”

expectations.text: Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: result.md and execution_notes.md both anchor to phase4a: “Phase alignment (primary: phase4a)” and “Meets phase4a contract intent… subcategory-only… scenarios and atomic nested steps,” and they note the phase4a contract constraints (subcategory-only, atomic steps, observable verification leaves; forbids canonical top-layer categories).
